name: _hash_dict
rationale: Called at cache.py:68 by Cache._get_cache_path_parts (same file). Cache's real subclass WheelCache is instantiated in commands/install.py:447, commands/wheel.py:115, commands/lock.py:115 — all three are Command implementations loaded only via commands/__init__.py's commands_dict: `module = importlib.import_module(module_path); command_class = getattr(module, class_name)` (string keys, not identifiers), so necro's import-graph resolver has no static edge into these command modules or anything they call, and marks the whole reachable subtree (including same-file callers) as unreferenced. Genuinely executed on every `pip install`/`wheel`/`lock` run.

name: autocomplete
rationale: Called directly at cli/main.py:67 (`autocomplete()`) inside `main()`, via a LOCAL import at main.py:47 (`from pip._internal.cli.autocompletion import autocomplete`). Genuinely executed on every real `pip` invocation, before any subcommand dispatch — this is upstream of the commands_dict boundary, not gated behind it, and (phase 48 fix) necro's Python entry resolution now correctly seeds `main()` itself as the prod entry (the pyproject-scripts spec `pip = "pip._internal.cli.main:main"` previously only seeded the bare file, never the `:main` function target, so `main()` and everything it calls read as unreachable).

name: get_path_completion_type
rationale: Called twice in the same file (autocompletion.py:90, 124) from within `autocomplete()`'s own real shell-completion logic — reachable via the same main()→autocomplete() chain as the `autocomplete` case above.

name: no_cache
rationale: A `partial(PipOption, ...)` option-factory referenced as a bare identifier inside `general_group`'s `"options"` list (cmdoptions.py:1267, same file). `general_group` is itself passed to `cmdoptions.make_option_group(cmdoptions.general_group, parser)` at cli/main_parser.py:36, inside `create_main_parser()`, which `parse_command()` calls at main_parser.py:72 — and `parse_command` is called directly from `main()` (cli/main.py, local import) to parse every real pip invocation's global `--no-cache-dir` option, upstream of command dispatch.

name: PipOption
rationale: A custom `optparse.Option` subclass referenced repeatedly as the class argument to `partial(PipOption, ...)` throughout cmdoptions.py's option definitions (e.g. lines 275, 364, 378), including `no_cache` and the other `general_group` members that are genuinely reached via `create_main_parser()` on every real invocation.

name: make_option_group
rationale: Called directly at cli/main_parser.py:36 (`cmdoptions.make_option_group(cmdoptions.general_group, parser)`) inside `create_main_parser()`, itself called from `parse_command()` — reached on every real pip invocation before any subcommand is selected.

name: raise_option_error
rationale: Called from multiple option-validation callbacks within the same file (cmdoptions.py:484, 810, 929) that back `general_group`'s option definitions — reached via the same create_main_parser()→general_group chain, exercised whenever a user passes an invalid value to one of pip's global CLI options.

name: get_similar_commands
rationale: Imported top-level and called at cli/main_parser.py:124 (`get_similar_commands(cmd_name)`) inside `parse_command()`'s "did you mean..." suggestion path when an unknown subcommand name is given — reached directly from `parse_command()`, upstream of the commands_dict dynamic-dispatch boundary that still gates the specific Command subclasses themselves.

name: ConfigOptionParser
rationale: Instantiated directly at cli/main_parser.py:24 (`parser = ConfigOptionParser(...)`) inside `create_main_parser()` — the real argparse-style parser object every pip invocation builds before any subcommand dispatch happens.

name: general_group
rationale: A module-level dict literal referenced across a file boundary at cli/main_parser.py:36 (`cmdoptions.general_group`) inside `create_main_parser()` — genuinely built and consumed on every real pip invocation to construct the global `--verbose`/`--quiet`/`--no-cache-dir`/etc. option group.

name: verbose
rationale: Another `general_group`-listed option (cmdoptions.py:1252, same file as its own declaration) backing the real `-v`/`--verbose` flag — reached via the same create_main_parser()→general_group chain as `no_cache` above; a second member of the same list to show the pattern isn't a one-off.

name: InstallCommand
rationale: The class instantiated by commands/__init__.py:create_command's `getattr(importlib.import_module(module_path), class_name)` on every `pip install` invocation — commands_dict maps the string "install" to this exact module_path/class_name pair. necro's `dynamic-import taint in scope` evidence correctly notices the pattern (hedges to `maybe` instead of `likely`), but the class is genuinely reachable, not merely uncertain.

name: HashCommand
rationale: Instantiated by commands_dict/create_command's dynamic getattr dispatch whenever `pip hash` runs. necro logs `no dynamic-import taint in scope` for this one (inconsistent with InstallCommand/WheelCommand above, which do get the taint hedge) — an interesting case where the same real mechanism is detected for some Command subclasses but not others.

name: FreezeCommand
rationale: Instantiated via commands_dict/create_command's dynamic getattr dispatch whenever `pip freeze` runs; same reachability gap as HashCommand — necro reports no taint hedge for this one either, landing purely on the exported-symbol quarantine to keep it at `maybe` rather than `likely`.

name: ShowCommand
rationale: Instantiated via commands_dict/create_command's dynamic getattr dispatch whenever `pip show` runs.

name: get_major_minor_version
rationale: Declared in locations/base.py but re-exported through locations/__init__.py's `__all__`/`from .base import (...)` chain (AC-1's target pattern). Consumed via the re-export path — `from pip._internal.locations import get_major_minor_version` — by utils/misc.py:36 and operations/install/wheel.py:38, not via a direct `.base` import, so this specifically exercises __init__.py re-export resolution rather than a same-file reference.

name: MaxLevelFilter
rationale: Never referenced by identifier anywhere — only by its fully-qualified dotted-path string "pip._internal.utils.logging.MaxLevelFilter" inside a logging.config-style dictConfig literal in the same file (utils/logging.py:332), which Python's stdlib `logging.config.dictConfig` resolves and instantiates dynamically at setup_logging() time. A string reference, not an identifier, so necro's identifier/attribute-based resolver cannot see it.

name: ExcludeLoggerFilter
rationale: Same dotted-path-string dynamic-instantiation pattern as MaxLevelFilter above (dictConfig literal at utils/logging.py:340), in the same file.

name: _has_option
rationale: Zero references anywhere in pip's real checkout (src/ or tests/) beyond its own declaration — confirmed by exhaustive grep across the full pinned-SHA checkout, not just the trimmed slice. vulture independently flags it (60% confidence, "unused function"). A scoped `pytest tests/unit/test_req_install.py` run to confirm via deletion was attempted but blocked in this sandbox by a missing test-only dependency (`installer`, pulled in by tests/conftest.py) unrelated to this symbol; the grep-based zero-reference confirmation across the complete checkout is the basis for this label.

name: today_is_later_than
rationale: A small standalone module-level helper (compares today's date to a given y/m/d) with zero references anywhere in the real checkout's src/ or tests/ trees. vulture independently flags it (60% confidence, "unused function"). Its sibling function in the same file, parse_iso_datetime, IS used elsewhere — this isn't a case of the whole file being unreached, just one genuinely vestigial helper.

name: HeadersFormatter
rationale: Passed by identifier into plugin_manager.register(...) in plugins/registry.py:17, which is imported top-level by httpie/core.py — reachable from the real entry point (httpie/__main__.py:main → `from httpie.core import main`, a LOCAL import inside __main__.py's main() function body). necro's Python import parser does record function-local imports as bindings, but even so this class is dead-flagged; the registration is a bare identifier passed as a call argument (not a decorator, not an attribute chain), confirmed live by grep across the full checkout with zero ambiguity.

name: JSONFormatter
rationale: Same plugin_manager.register(...) list-argument registration in plugins/registry.py:18 as HeadersFormatter above, reachable from httpie/core.py.

name: XMLFormatter
rationale: Same plugin_manager.register(...) list-argument registration in plugins/registry.py:19 as HeadersFormatter above.

name: SessionNameValidator
rationale: Instantiated at cli/definition.py:583 (`session_name_validator = SessionNameValidator(...)`), which is part of the real argparse option-definition module built at import time by httpie's CLI parser.

name: response_charset_type
rationale: Passed as the `type=` callback for the --response-charset argparse option at cli/definition.py:352 — a genuine, exercised reference, just not a call-site necro's resolver associates with the declaration.

name: SEPARATOR_PROXY
rationale: Used at cli/definition.py:719 as `KeyValueArgType(SEPARATOR_PROXY)` while building the --proxy option's argument type — imported and referenced across a file boundary via a plain top-level `from ... import (...)` (cli/definition.py:16).

name: SORTED_FORMAT_OPTIONS_STRING
rationale: Used at cli/definition.py:282 and in --help text at definition.py:345, imported top-level at definition.py:17.

name: generate_subparsers
rationale: Called at manager/cli.py:181, module-level code in the same file that builds the `httpie` management-CLI's subcommand parser at import time — genuinely executed whenever the `httpie`/`httpie.manager` console script runs (setup.cfg's `httpie = httpie.manager.__main__:main` entry point), a separate entry point from `http`/`https`.

name: RE_STATUS_LINE
rationale: Used at http.py:26 in the same file's status-line regex matcher, part of the real HTTP-response syntax highlighter (SimplifiedHTTPLexer, see below).

name: SimplifiedHTTPLexer
rationale: Imported and instantiated via a LOCAL import inside ColorFormatter's own method body — `from ..lexers.http import SimplifiedHTTPLexer` at output/formatters/colors.py:71 — not a top-level import, so this specifically exercises the function-local-import binding path (same underlying reachability mechanism as the `httpie/__main__.py:main()`-style local imports that gate the whole app's entry chain).

name: PREFIX_TOKEN
rationale: Used at json.py:26 (`bygroups(PREFIX_TOKEN, using(JsonLexer))`) in the same file's JSON-body syntax highlighter, part of httpie's real output-formatting path.

name: _update_checker
rationale: Applied as a decorator — `@_update_checker` — to check_updates (update_warnings.py:140), in the same file. check_updates is imported top-level and called directly by httpie/core.py:98 inside main(), which is reachable from the real entry point. The decorator-application identifier is a genuine, exercised reference necro's graph does not credit.

name: FETCH_INTERVAL
rationale: Used at update_warnings.py:71, same file, inside maybe_fetch_updates (see below) — a plain same-file module-scope constant reference.

name: maybe_fetch_updates
rationale: Called at update_warnings.py:102, same file, from the wrapper() closure that _update_checker's decorator installs around check_updates — reachable transitively from httpie/core.py:98's check_updates(env) call on every real httpie invocation (update checks are throttled internally by this function, not skipped).

name: SEPARATOR_GROUP_DATA_EMBED_ITEMS
rationale: A frozenset constant with zero references anywhere else in the real checkout (src or tests) beyond its own declaration — confirmed by exhaustive grep across the full pinned-SHA checkout.

name: SEPARATORS
rationale: Module-level constant built from SEPARATOR_GROUP_ALL_ITEMS at import time but never read anywhere else in the checkout — dead computed constant, not merely an unused import.

name: STYLE_PROGRAM_NAME
rationale: Declared alongside STYLE_METAVAR/STYLE_SWITCH/etc (all real, used styles) but this one specific style constant has zero references anywhere else in the checkout — a genuinely vestigial sibling among otherwise-live constants, not a whole-file reachability gap.

name: LEFT_PADDING_5
rationale: Declared alongside LEFT_PADDING_2/3/4 (all real, used elsewhere) but LEFT_PADDING_5 itself has zero references anywhere else in the checkout.

name: to_help_message
rationale: A module-level function with zero references anywhere else in the real checkout. vulture independently flags it (60% confidence, "unused function").

name: render_as_string
rationale: A module-level helper for rendering a rich renderable to a plain string, with zero references anywhere else in the real checkout. vulture independently flags it (60% confidence).

name: enable_highlighter
rationale: A contextmanager helper with zero references anywhere else in the real checkout. vulture independently flags it (60% confidence).

name: DEFAULT_SESSIONS_DIR
rationale: A module-level constant (default sessions directory path) with zero references anywhere else in the real checkout, including tests — the sessions feature resolves its directory through a different path elsewhere.

name: __date__
rationale: Module metadata dunder (release date string) declared alongside __version__ — but unlike __version__ (imported and used in 8+ places: core.py, client.py, config.py, cli/definition.py, manager/cli.py, tests), __date__ has zero references anywhere in the real checkout. A dunder-named module attribute is exported by convention (isExported's DUNDER_NAME rule), but exported-by-convention doesn't mean referenced — this pair is a direct alive/dead contrast within the same declaration block.

name: __licence__
rationale: Same situation as __date__ above (misspelled deliberately by upstream as "licence" not "license") — zero references anywhere in the real checkout.

name: __hash__
rationale: A dunder-method protocol case: _InstallRequirementBackedCandidate.__hash__ (with its paired __eq__ at line 184) is never called by name anywhere in the checkout — it's invoked implicitly by the Python interpreter whenever a Candidate instance is inserted into a set/dict, which resolvelib's real resolution algorithm does genuinely at runtime (e.g. `explicit_candidates: set[Candidate] = set()` in factory.py:458, populated during every real `pip install` dependency resolution). No textual call site exists to find; reachability is real but only visible via the implicit hashing/equality protocol, not a traceable reference edge.

name: yield_lines
rationale: A functools.singledispatch decorator-registered function (`@functools.singledispatch def yield_lines(...)`, with a str-specialized handler registered via `@yield_lines.register(str)` at line 59) — the decorator-registration hard pattern. This entire module's own docstring says it verbatim: "These functions are not supposed to be used within pip._internal." Confirmed by exhaustive grep across the full pinned-SHA checkout (src/ and tests/): no import of pip._internal.utils._jaraco_text exists anywhere — the module (and its sibling drop_comment, join_continuation, _nonblank) is entirely vestigial, left over from a vendoring exercise. necro's own scan (see pip-scan.json) does flag yield_lines as verdict=dead, but only at tier=maybe rather than likely — this is a live instance of the phase-47 library-quarantine/recall tension noted in T1's handoff: the file lives under a package whose pyproject.toml triggers the library exported-symbol quarantine, so a genuinely, unambiguously dead top-level function is held at maybe instead of likely, directly illustrating the recall-floor risk T2 exists to measure.
